title: Coded-Exposure Imaging for Motion Deblur
sub_domain: Temporal coding for motion robust imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in the Raskar-Agrawal-Tumblin 2006 coded-exposure paper and follow-up deblurring literature.

E.description: A shutter opens and closes according to a binary temporal code c(t) during a single exposure T, so the camera integral of a moving scene is a convolution by a broadband temporal kernel h_c(t) = c(t) instead of a low-pass box h_box(t) = rect(t/T). The broadband kernel preserves high spatial frequencies of the moving object, making invertible motion deblur possible.
E.forward_model: y(u,v) = integral_0^T c(t) * x(u - v_t * t, v) dt + n(u,v)   (1D motion along u-axis at velocity v_t)
E.world_state_x: Sharp scene x(u,v) in R^{H x W} and (optionally) motion velocity v_t
E.observation_y: Motion-blurred image y(u,v) with broadband temporal PSF imprint
physical_parameters_theta: Shutter code c(t) in {0,1}^T: binary on/off sequence over T (typ. 52-bit MURA or Raskar-optimal)

W.regime: Coded kernel h_c is chosen to have approximately flat magnitude response (MURA or optimal binary codes); OTF is nonzero across full passband, so Wiener deconvolution is well-conditioned. Stability dominated by motion-direction / velocity mismatch and saturation clipping.

C.solver_class: Wiener deconvolution, blind-deconv (BRISQ-conv, Fergus), learned (DeepDeblur, MPRNet when coded prior known)
C.error_bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma / sqrt(T) + C2 * velocity_error + C3 * direction_error; OTF magnitude sets per-frequency amplification
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(H*W)) per iteration (1D FFT along motion axis)
